If you would like to challenge the accuracy of your company's safety data, you can do so using FMCSA's [DataQs](http://dataqs.fmcsa.dot.gov/) system. **USDOT Status** - **ACTIVE**: The entity's US DOT number is active. - **INACTIVE**: Inactive per 49 CFR 390.19(b)(4); biennial update of MCS-150 data not completed. - **OUT-OF-SERVICE**: Carrier is under any type of out-of-service order and is not authorized to operate. **Operating Authority Status** - **AUTHORIZED FOR { Passenger, Property, HHG }**: This will list the specific operating authorities the carrier (or broker) is allowed to operate. - **NOT AUTHORIZED**: The entity does not have any operating authority and/or is not authorized to engage in interstate, for-hire operations. \*Please Note:NOT AUTHORIZEDdoes not apply to Private or Intrastate operations. - **OUT-OF-SERVICE**: Carrier is under any type of out-of-service order and is not authorized to operate. **Out of Service Date** Indicates the date the company was ordered Out of Service. If there are multiple Out of Service orders, the earliest date will be displayed. Please note:If there are multiple Out-of-Service orders, the earliest date will be displayed. For help on the explanation of individual data fields, click on any field name or for help of a general nature go to [**SAFER General Help**](https://safer.fmcsa.dot.gov/saferhelp.aspx#General). **The information below reflects the content of the FMCSA management information systems as of** **07/01/2025.
